heading: Learning algorithms shouldn't feel like guesswork

cards.statement: Reading pseudocode doesn't show you why it works
cards.description: Walls of code and abstract notation leave you guessing at the intuition behind every step.

cards.statement: Static diagrams freeze the moment — they can't show state changes
cards.description: A single snapshot can't capture how data transforms at each iteration or recursive call.

cards.statement: Misunderstanding one algorithm costs hours of interview prep
cards.description: One shaky concept compounds — you reach a mock interview unable to reason through the solution.

cards.statement: Switching between a textbook and a debugger breaks your focus
cards.description: Context-switching between theory and a bare REPL makes it hard to connect the two.

transitionLine: There's a better way to build the intuition that sticks.
